proposed_experiment_variables.electrode_rotation_rate: The rotational speed (e.g., in rpm) of the working electrode (such as a rotating disk or cylinder) used to quantitatively control hydrodynamic mass transport conditions and diffusion layer thickness at the electrode-electrolyte interface during the electrochemical test.
proposed_experiment_variables.electrochemical_conditioning_protocol: The specific in-situ pre-polarization parameters (e.g., applied cathodic potential and duration) used to precondition the working electrode prior to the main test, typically performed to reduce native air-formed oxides and establish a reproducible standard surface state.
